title: Prefetch, Goroutine Concurrency, and Capacity · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for prefetch, goroutine concurrency, and capacity and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a measured consumer-capacity budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Interpret consumer capacity with delivery rate, processing latency, ready and unacked depth, dependency saturation, and queue single-core limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Prefetch Window Semantics · changed case · Quiz Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 4608 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Prefetch Window Semantics without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fair Tenant Dispatch
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Fair Tenant Dispatch: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 4326 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fair Tenant Dispatch.
steps.why: Retrieving Fair Tenant Dispatch through the concrete a measured consumer-capacity budget case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fair Tenant Dispatch, keep Fair Tenant Dispatch connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fair Tenant Dispatch, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Fair Tenant Dispatch precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fair Tenant Dispatch, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One shared FIFO queue guarantees fair service across tenants and task costs. In Go, unbounded goroutines compensate for an oversized prefetch safely.
